name: omarchy_neovim
content:
Neovim 
Neovim is a modern impementation of the vi editor created by Bi Joy a the way back in
1976. It's a moda editor where insert mode and command mode are separated, and it's a
bit of a superpower once you earn even just a subset of the incrediby deep key
command set. But it's aso quite the earning curve!
If you're totay new to vim-stye editing, I recommend you checkout ThePrimeagen's Vim
As Your Editor series on YouTube. That' teach you the basics. Just know that unike more
simiar mainstream editors, it's going to take you onger to get basic proficiency with vim.
But once you do, the payoff is aso arger.
Now Neovim is basicay infinitey configurabe. If you reay want to go nuts, you can
create your own Neovim configuration from scratch. There's a great course from
Typecraft on setting up Neovim from scratch. And ThePrimegean has one as we.
But Omarchy ships with a compete Neovim setup that's been ovingy tuned to showcase
the best of what's possibe out of the box. Without you having to write a singe ine of
configuration! It's caed LazyVim, and it's a distribution of Neovim pugins and
configurations. It's awesome.
LazyVim Basics 
As mentioned, I'm not going to teach you vim in this short introduction, but I can show you
a few basics of LazyVim, and how to get around.
First, Neovim has the idea of the eader key. That's basicay the gateway to a the
commands. LazyVim has set that to Space . So just press that, wait a second, and you'
see a bunch of options expained inine ike this:
Here are some basic commands I use a the time:
• Space Space  - Fuzzy-find any fie in the current directory.
• Space S G  - Search a fies using grep with a preview.
• Space E  - Togge the fie tree on/off.
• Ctrl + W W  - Hop from the fie tree to the editor and back.
• Shift + H  - Move eft between the open tabs (vim cas them buffers).
• Shift + L  - Move right between the open tabs.
• Space B D  - Cose a tab.
• Space B O  - Cose a other tabs but the current.
• Space G G  - Launch LazyGit in a foating pane from the current directory.
• Space U W  - Togge soft wrap.
Whie you're in the fie tree (Space E  to revea, Ctrl + W W  to hop over there), you can
add a new fie with a  or a new directory with A . Press ?  whie in the tree to see a
commands.
Neovim · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/56/neovim

---

If you want to pickup the basic vim anguage, I've written about the three-part syntax and
how to pu off those sick combo moves!
You can see a the possibe commands on the LazyVim Keymaps page.
Starting Neovim 
You can start Neovim using Super + Shift + N, but it's usuay easier to drive it from the
termina by navigating to the directory you wish to work in and typing n . The n  is the
aias for nvim , which wi use the the present directory to open by defaut. You can open a
singe fie with n myfile.txt .
Using Neovim for sudo edits 
If you need to edit fies that you can ony change as a super user, you can use neovim with
a your pugins setup by running sudoedit /etc/sudoers.d/00-sudo-only-file.
